title: Fragrant Donabe Crispy Malaysian Tofu Pilaf
description: A delicate clay-pot rice dish featuring golden tofu and tangy capers, infused with ginger and turmeric for a light, sattvic-friendly meal.
instructions: Heat the coconut oil in the donabe over medium heat and sear the tofu cubes until golden and crispy.
Remove the tofu from the pot and set aside while leaving the remaining oil.
Stir the grated ginger and turmeric into the oil until fragrant.
Add the soaked rice and capers to the donabe, stirring to coat every grain with the spiced oil.
Pour in the vegetable stock and salt, then bring the liquid to a gentle simmer.
Cover the donabe with its lid and reduce the heat to the lowest setting.
Cook the rice for 15 minutes without lifting the lid to maintain the steam.
Turn off the heat and let the pot sit undisturbed for another 10 minutes to finish steaming.
Gently fold the crispy tofu back into the rice using a wooden paddle.
Drizzle with lime juice and garnish with fresh cilantro before serving directly from the clay pot.
